 3 ## [1/61] Hengky Sucanda
 4 Source: gchat | Group: dm/_gwJCgAAAAE | Relevance: 9/10 | Mention: direct | Updated: 2026-04-21T02:24:39.179000+00:00 | Work Items: CoMall, fpg-oneapp-bff | People: Michael Bui, Hengky Sucanda | Labels: deployment-fix, pipeline-ops, repository-mgmt, peer-coord, build-stability
 5 On April 20, 2026, Michael Bui initiated a direct conversation with Hengky Sucanda regarding a critical deployment failure in the Bitbucket pipeline for the 'CoMall' project. The issue stemmed from errors in generating API documentation within the `fpg-oneapp-bff` repository, which forced Michael to pin repositories to an older commit to maintain delivery continuity. In response, Hengky apologized and immediately implemented a fix on April 21 at 02:16 UTC by modifying the build process to handle errors silently, ensuring future builds would not fail. The fix was merged into the master branch that same day, allowing Michael to restore the pipeline to use the latest commit. This interaction represents a high-priority operational unblocking event involving direct peer coordination, specific repository management, and resolution of a blocking technical debt issue for a key retail e-commerce initiative.
 6

 9 In the [Internal] (Ecom/Omni) Digital Product Development chat regarding ticket DPD-838, Chee Hoe Leong asked Michael Bui to confirm if the ENV_SECRET_SPLITIO_API_CREDS were updated for production and inquired about further PR changes. Michael responded confirming the credentials were updated for the PROD environment. This interaction falls under Production incidents or SLO breaches/service configuration updates within services Michael governs. 11 ## [3/61] RMN Integration
12 Source: gchat | Group: space/AAQAKHz7DQM/FJT6Am_cKek | Relevance: 9/10 | Mention: direct | Updated: 2026-04-21T05:30:42.951000+00:00 | Work Items: RMN Integration, Ad Service API Contract | People: Michael Bui, Stella Liu, Tiong Siong Tee, Nikhil Grover, Vera Wijaya, Alvin Choo | Labels: api-integration, ad-services, pagination, uat-testing, technical-specs
13 In the RMN Integration Google Chat thread dated April 20-21, 2026, Michael Bui leads technical discussions with Stella Liu regarding the implementation of a new pagination API for ad services deployed to UAT. Michael provided detailed specifications on using viewUUID, offset, page, and quantity parameters to prevent duplicate ads during scrolling. He reviewed Stella's JSON payload samples, correcting specific data mapping issues: adjusting store IDs, clarifying banner quantities (confirming max 20), and standardizing ad unit names (e.g., changing HOME_R4U_1 to HOME_R4U). The conversation confirmed that the new API design consolidates homepage component requests into a single call to improve performance. Michael explicitly addressed technical questions about screen reloading logic and tab-based navigation, validating that the unified request approach is correct for the current implementation.
14 
15 ## [4/61] RMN Integration
16 Source: gchat | Group: space/AAQAKHz7DQM/MeKqRqJLM5U | Relevance: 9/10 | Mention: direct | Updated: 2026-04-21T08:18:59.273000+00:00 | Work Items: RMN Integration | People: Stella Liu, Michael Bui | Labels: ad-technology, architecture-decision, front-end-fix, data-ordering, technical-governance
17 In a discussion regarding the RMN (Retail Media Network) integration on April 21, 2026, Stella Liu sought clarification from Michael Bui regarding the front-end display order of products. She asked if the system should present items in ascending order of position based on a provided image. Michael Bui directly responded with an architectural correction, instructing the team to ignore the 'position' field in the response object entirely. He specified that the front-end must instead adhere strictly to the sequential order of ads as they appear within the main array/list. This interaction represents a critical technical design decision affecting the ad rendering logic and product catalog implementation.
18 
19 ## [5/61] DPD x Platform Engineering

21 On April 21, 2026, the DPD x Platform Engineering Google Chat focused on resolving production Redis memory spikes in zs-fpon-prd. Wai Ching Chan confirmed completion of cleanup for db2 keys with missing TTLs and sought identification for db0 key owners to address a continuing spike. Daryl Ng identified RMN as the user of db0 keys. The discussion centers on addressing root causes identified by Michael Bui regarding excessive TTLs (>1 year) in db0, missing TTLs in db2, and sudden key count spikes. This aligns with Michael's governance mandate to prioritize structural code remediation over temporary capacity increases. While no specific Jira tickets were created in this snippet, the conversation is a direct escalation of production reliability issues requiring architectural judgment and cross-team coordination (RMN team) to prevent SLO breaches.

While Tiong Siong initially suggested starting earlier is better than later, Michael confirmed the sprint deadline. The team debated whether to track tasks on Jira or a local team board; they decided against using the 'LIGHT' board (reserved for Comall) and agreed to assign tasks to efficient personnel within their own team board. This conversation touches on Engineering Governance (SLO/metrics via Sonar), Process-oriented workflows (Agile sprint management), and Stakeholder coordination regarding task assignment.
